caption:
Welcome to our new channel, a dedicated spot for all things Flow. You'll find product updates, best practices, behind-the-scenes stories from filmmakers and the team and, of course, an abundance of videos created in Flow. 

Join us on this creative adventure and #FindyourFlow

You have generated over 100M videos in Flow 🤯. We are SO grateful for your continued enthusiasm + support. As a token of our appreciation, here are two updates:

1.) AI credits are DOUBLED for all Ultra users
2.) We're launching @FlowbyGoogle, your new source for Flow tips and news

Oh, our header image? Here's a fun tip on how to use storyboards to create videos in Flow. Using Frames to Video, upload your storyboards (hand-drawn or digital), add a simple prompt, and Flow will understand your creative vision and bring it to life.

Try it and share them here!
In filmmaking, consistency is key to a strong narrative. Using Flow, you can build stories with consistent characters, scenes, and objects across multiple clips— giving you more creative control.

(1/5) Maintain consistency as you execute your creative vision. 🎥

With Ingredients to Video, you can create impactful stories, while maintaining consistent characters across multiple scenes. 

So, now we can follow the same main characters (a man, his balloon, and a snazzy green and pink car) on an expanding adventure in the Mojave Desert…

Keeping characters consistent in your AI films can be difficult. Flow's Ingredients to Video feature makes character consistency a breeze - and better yet, it's now available to both Ultra AND Pro users! 🔥

We are now offering all new users 100 credits per month free of charge to try Flow (the equivalent of 1 Veo 3 Quality video or 5 Veo 3 Fast videos).

Go beyond the single clip — trim and extend your scenes (with consistency!) in http://flow.google today.

Oh the details... they can really make or break a story. But don't you worry, we got your back. 🤝

Beyond character consistency, what about that iconic prop? Flow's Ingredients to Video feature allows you to keep objects consistent across frames. So that bedazzled bag can get the air time it deserves.

Some stories are meant for the big screen. Others are designed for the phone screen. Flow now supports vertical formats, making it easier to create stunning videos for mobile and social sharing📱.

Try it today and share your favorite creations below!

Hopefully it was worth the wait 😉 

We always appreciate your feedback, let us know what other features you'd like to see in Flow!

From towering castles to dramatic low-angle shots, capture the full scale of your scene with these new vertical formats.

What are you most excited to create?

(1/7) As an AI filmmaking tool built with and for creatives, we wanted to put our features to the test. So, we created our own short film!

Follow this thread for a behind-the-scenes look at the creative process, and see how Flow can help you build stories with consistent characters, scenes, and objects.

Already have an image you'd like to bring to life? Use it as your first frame and watch the story change with each new annotation you add.

Annotation: “We zoom out a lot and discover this is a three wall set in the middle of a forest.”

By sketching over your images and using Frames to Video, you can transform those hard-to-articulate ideas into reality.

Annotations: “Some books fall to the floor.” / “The woman steps back.” / “A train speeds through the corridor.”

This new feature... it's bananas 🍌

You can now edit and refine your images directly in Flow using prompts. 
@NanoBanana
 maintains the likeness of a subject or scene across different lighting, environments, artistic styles, and more.

See the a-peel for yourself— try it today!

Same prompt, different looks 💁🏻‍♀️

Introducing Prompt Expanders! This new feature uses Gemini to enhance your prompts to create a specific style of video. Starting with a few fun presets like Action Figure, Film Noir, and Cinematic — see the range of what Flow is able to do!

Hungry for more? Create your own custom Expander that you can reuse across a variety of clips, giving you even more control and consistency across your story. For example:

— Describe a specific style or genre, like a live-action musical, and Flow will help maintain it as you create videos.
— Describe a character, then, whenever you reference them in a prompt, Flow will know what that character should look like.
— Have all of your characters speak in a certain accent, like old English, then plug and play to create your dream film.

Try it out and hopefully you'll be singing praises (like in the example below):
Use First and Last Frame to dial in your opening and closing shots. And now you can bridge those frames together with a custom soundtrack or dialogue, ensuring your story flows as you intended.

🥳 The launches continue with a brand new feature: Editing your videos directly in Flow. Starting with object insertion, just select where you want your object and describe it. Flow will then both generate the object *and* seamlessly integrate it into your clip.

Ask and you shall receive: our beloved doodle feature is officially available in Flow! 

Just tap the pencil icon on any of your images. From there, you can draw, type, or annotate to your heart's desire. Finally, a simple prompt like, "follow the instructions in my annotations, then remove my annotations" will bring your masterpiece to life.

Since we launched our free of charge tier, we've been SO inspired by your creations (and want to see even more). So for a limited time, we're expanding monthly credits for the free tier from 100 to 180.

Hopefully this enables new & existing users to explore what's possible in Flow!

Frames to Video, powered by Veo 3.1, has led to even more creative control. Being able to decide your first and last frames lets you focus on perfecting the details in between.

Share some of your favorite Frames to Video clips below!

Something we love about using Ingredients to Video with Veo 3.1 is how easy it can be to maintain consistency across different settings, cinematic styles, and even... users?

Let's test it out: Use the ingredient we provided and decide where the character will go next!

Introducing Camera Adjustment! A new experimental feature, just tap the pencil icon to adjust the camera position, orbit, or move the "dolly" in any of your generated videos. Currently available to Flow AI Ultra Subscribers (and FREE of charge for a limited time!).
When creating with real-world objects, like a branded product, try using multiple views of your product as ingredients to capture the details of every angle.

1️⃣ Asset generation

Generate clean, isolated asset sheets with compositional precision for finalizing poses into a visual masterpiece.

Prompt: I need a photorealistic character sheet of this person: 4 different poses, horizontal arrangement, 16:9 frame, on white background.

10 things you can do with Nano Banana Pro in Flow to help with your visual storytelling.

First, start with your character, which you can use as an ingredient for consistency across shots. 🧵

Creativity is rarely a straight line, it often takes us in unexpected directions. That’s why we built a suite of tools designed to help you refine, edit, and pivot your vision as it evolves.

Whether you're making a small tweak or reimagining an entire scene, Flow helps bridge the gap between your initial concept and the final output.

Let’s jump into a workflow below 👇
Here's another way to elevate your storytelling using Frames to Video: Use the same first and last frame to hold your setting consistent. One setup, endless stories.  #FindYourFlow

Whether you’re creating ingredients, frames, or stunning visuals, you need the highest resolution images possible. That's why we've introduced upscaling for images in Flow. Now you can download Nano Banana Pro image generations in ultra-high resolution (2K & 4K).

Our new state-of-the-art video upscaling allows you to download videos in 1080p and, for the first time, in 4K!

💡 Pro Tip: Want the absolute highest fidelity? Combine our upscaling tools.

1. Download a generated image in 4K 

2. Upload it as an Ingredient

3. Turn it into a stunning 1080p or 4K video

Better inputs = better outputs. The results have never been clearer. ✨

Use the prompt below to generate a new surprise ending by replacing the [SUBJECT] field. Show us the marvels you generate at the end of the tunnel!

“Shaky handheld domestic handycam footage. Continuous one shot walking in a dark black narrow cavern looking everywhere, just walking. We keep walking through the narrow dark tunnel. Suddenly there is a curve in the tunnel that turns left. We keep walking and turn left, nearly at 6 second, after the curve, we look up to discover a [SUBJECT] carefully carved on the stone. Shaky handheld, blurry, chaotic, soft natural colors, ultra detailed, blurry.”

Sometimes the best way to communicate your vision is to draw it. ✍️

Watch how we used a rough sketch to lock-in the composition, Nano Banana Pro to refine the look, and then Frames to Video to bring it all together. #FindYourFlow

New Veo update! Here's what's new:

— Veo 3.1 Ingredients to Video now supports vertical formats with a more expressive model

— State-of-the-art video upscaling to 1080p and 4K

Veo 3.1 is going vertical. 🤳

We’ve updated Ingredients to Video with native portrait support, and more expressive storytelling! Upload your own images and create stories to match ✨ 

Plus: SOTA 1080p & 4K upscaling 🎞️ in Flow & APIs

Try it in @GeminiApp, @YouTube, @FlowbyGoogle, or @GoogleAIStudio

This level of detail is honestly… *wild* 🦁

Create your own videos with fluid movements using this prompt:

“High detailed macro shot, shot on movie camera, soft natural grading, contrasted lights, swift camera follow motion, chaotic. Lateral blurred macro shot following a [SUBJECT] running in [LOCATION]”

With 4K upscaling for both images and videos in Flow, your final outputs can look sharper than ever.

Here’s a simple workflow that shows how you can create clearer inputs, to get clearer outputs.

Sketch. Visualize. Animate. ✨

Take a rough sketch, tweak and generate the end-product with Nano Banana Pro and turn it into a polished video using Frames to Video.

If you’ve created characters you love, try bringing them to life in a story with Ingredients to Video in vertical format.

Can these two really be close friends? 🎈🌵

Exploring a visual style is one of the most exciting parts of the creative process. Color grading is a powerful way to define the atmosphere and give your story its own signature. #FindYourFlow

A simple way to maintain your color grading throughout your story is by…
1️⃣ Including “match the color grading” in your prompt when generating your first or last frames with Nano Banana Pro in Flow.
2️⃣ Then, explore new settings and scenes while maintaining your look across clips.
3️⃣ Finally, use Frames to Video to animate your scenes.

Here’s a look at the workflow:

Quickly move from sketch to video in Flow to test an idea: 

1️⃣ Rough sketch to lock the composition
2️⃣ Nano Banana Pro to refine the aesthetic
3️⃣ Frames to Video to bring it to life

Prompts added below!

Setting isn't just a backdrop; it’s a character.

Bring the details of your worlds to life in Flow, no matter how fantastical or outlandish they may be.

Turn your "what if" into "look at this."

Draw your concept down on paper, take a photo, upload it into Flow, describe your vision to create your frame using Nano Banana Pro, and then prompt it into action with Frames to Video.

Nano Banana Pro image creation 🤝 Frames to Video: A dynamic duo.

Aside from giving you a glimpse into your pet’s secret double-life, Nano Banana Pro really lets you focus in on the details, all before even generating your video. For example:

📸 Start with that great pet picture you'd like to animate.
🏙️ Use Nano Banana Pro in Flow to help tweak the setting or add a prop.
⚡️ Bring it all to life with Frames to Video.

Don’t hold Fido back - let him fly. 🏍️

Debuting today!

We’re live on Instagram to share extra visual inspiration and put a spotlight on the incredible work you’re all creating with Flow.

Find us here: https://instagram.com/flowbygoogle
Shift your perspective 🦅 #MadeWithFlow #GoogleFlow
Carry onward ➡️ #MadeWithFlow #GoogleFlow
Pause for reflection 🪞 #MadeWithFlow #GoogleFlow

Welcome!

As Flow - our AI filmmaking tool - has evolved, so has our community. We’re opening this space to share inspiration, learn helpful tips, and grow together.

Tag us in your creative work and we’d love to share it with the rest of the community! And follow along for more exciting updates to come soon. ✨

#MadewithFlow #GoogleFlow
Go deeper into your creative explorations. #MadeWithFlow #GoogleFlow
Dive into your creative pursuits. #MadeWithFlow #GoogleFlow
Find delight in your creative details. #MadeWithFlow #GoogleFlow
